summary.intent: Implement all 6 findings from Codex round-3 review of a color picker component, including a user-decided HSB-saturation infrastructure addition and 5 clear bug fixes.
summary.outcome: All 6 findings implemented: added `ColorValue::from_hsb` to ars-core, space-aware area editing via `AreaXStep`/`AreaYStep` events and `set_area`/`area_axes` helpers, disabled guard for `ChangeColorSpace`, eyedropper `type="button"`, `data-ars-dragging` scoped to area drags, HSB alpha in `value_as_string`, and channel inputs with `Value`. Spec synced end-to-end, 97 tests + new ars-core round-trip test pass, clippy clean, coverage gate passes, committed and pushed.

repo: `ColorChannel::Saturation` and `with_channel(Saturation)` operate on HSL saturation only — there is no HSB-saturation setter or `from_hsb` constructor in ars-core; HSB area editing requires new infrastructure.
The ars-components coverage gate enforces ≥90% branch coverage; landing exactly at 90.0% is fragile and needs active headroom management.
Codex review runs in numbered rounds; each round produces a batch of threaded findings that must be replied to and resolved after the corresponding fixes are pushed.
`fmin` is already in scope in `crates/ars-core/src/color.rs` (used by `to_hsb`), so no new import is needed for `from_hsb`.

code.finding: Added `ColorValue::from_hsb(h, s, b, a)` constructor; `fmin` already in scope from `to_hsb`. A round-trip test was added in the same file.

Added `AreaXStep`/`AreaYStep` to the Event enum; replaced `apply_drag_position` with space-aware `set_area` + `area_axes` helpers; made `set_channel_value`/`channel_current` space-aware for Saturation in HSB mode; scoped `data-ars-dragging` to area drags only; added `type="button"` to eyedropper; set `Value` on channel inputs with per-channel formatting; added `ChangeColorSpace` to the disabled guard.

`matches!(… if …)` in test assertions adds uncovered branches that reduce branch coverage. Converting to `assert_eq!` (with a local `channel_step_default` computation) is branch-free and restores coverage headroom.

workflow: After a coverage gate lands at exactly the threshold, convert any `matches!(… if …)` test guards to `assert_eq!` to eliminate the implicit false-branch and restore headroom before pushing.
Spec files must be synced in the same commit batch as code changes: Types block, events enum (§1.2), machine helpers/guard (§1.7), connect highlights (§1.8), and anatomy table (§2) all need updates.
Run `spec validate` + snippet compile as a final gate after spec edits — validates before committing rather than discovering breakage in CI.
Background coverage gate runs (`ScheduleWakeup`) allow commit/push prep to proceed in parallel; re-read the output file on wake before acting.

friction: `with_channel(Saturation)` being HSL-only made the round-2 HSB area implementation incoherent (x→HSL-sat, y→HSB-brightness) — this was not caught until Codex round-3 and required a design decision from the user before work could continue.
Coverage gate landed at exactly 90.0% branch after the initial round-3 fixes, requiring a second coverage run and guard-conversion pass before the commit was safe to push.
Extensive multi-pass reads of `color_picker/mod.rs` were needed to locate edit anchors due to the file's size — several sequential read+edit cycles for the same file.
open_items: Reply to and resolve all 6 Codex round-3 threads on the PR after pushing (mentioned as the next step but not shown as completed in the transcript).
Re-trigger `@codex review` for a potential round-4 pass after the round-3 fixes land.
ars-components branch coverage is at exactly the threshold floor after round-3 — any future branch-introducing change needs to either add covering tests or will require another headroom fix.
